title: What is POS Tagging?
concept: Part-of-Speech tagging labels each word in a sentence with its grammatical role (e.g. noun, verb, adjective) based on context.

exercises.prompt: Why do we want to label words as nouns, verbs, or adjectives in computer programs?

options.text: To help computers understand the grammatical structure of sentences.
To translate sentences into binary code instantly.
To check if words are spelled correctly.

exercises.explain: Labeling words by their grammatical roles helps computers understand the structure and meaning of human sentences.

cards.title: Double Trouble Words
cards.body: If you tell an AI 'I saw a giant saw,' how does it know the first 'saw' is something you did with your eyes, and the second 'saw' is a sharp tool? POS Tagging solves this mystery!

cards.title: What is POS Tagging?
cards.body: POS (Part-of-Speech) Tagging is an AI process that automatically reads a sentence and labels every single word with its correct grammatical role, like Noun, Verb, or Adjective, based on how it is used.

cards.title: The Sentence Movie Set
cards.body: Think of a sentence like a movie set. Every word is an actor, and POS Tagging is the director handing out scripts. The word 'run' could play the role of an Action (Verb) in one scene, or a Thing (Noun) in a 'home run' in another scene!

cards.title: Tagging in Action
cards.body:
Take the sentence: 'The happy dog barked.'
An AI using POS tagging will label it like this:
• The -> Determiner (DET)
• happy -> Adjective (ADJ)
• dog -> Noun (NOUN)
• barked -> Verb (VERB)

cards.title: Myth: Words Only Have One Role
cards.body: People think a word only has one fixed label (like 'run' is always a verb). In reality, words change their roles depending on the sentence. POS tagging decides the label dynamically based on context!

cards.title: One Word, Two Tags
cards.body: See how the same word 'fly' gets two completely different tags depending on its neighbors!

right.text: Birds fly high in the sky.

left.text: There is a fly on the wall.

exercises.prompt: In NLP and language technology, what does the abbreviation 'POS' stand for?
exercises.explain: POS stands for 'Part of Speech', representing grammatical categories like nouns, verbs, and adjectives.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A POS Tagger only labels the very first word of a sentence.
exercises.explain: POS Tagging assigns a label (like Noun, Verb, etc.) to every single word in a sentence, not just the first one.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of POS Tagging.
exercises.sentence: POS Tagging needs to look at the surrounding ________ of a word to decide its correct grammatical label.
exercises.explain: The surrounding words provide the context that helps the AI decide if a word is acting as a noun, verb, or something else.

exercises.prompt: An AI is looking for sentences where the word 'light' is tagged as an ADJECTIVE. Which sentence should it choose?
exercises.explain: The third sentence uses 'light' to describe the bag, making it an Adjective. The others use 'light' as a Noun (the physical light) or a Verb (to light a candle).

exercises.prompt: Imagine an AI reads the sentences 'I cook dinner' and 'He is a great cook'. How does POS Tagging know that the first 'cook' is a Verb, but the second 'cook' is a Noun?

options.text: It looks up the word in a simple dictionary without reading the rest of the sentence.
It looks at the surrounding words to see how the word is being used in that specific sentence.
It counts the number of letters in the word to guess its role.
It randomly selects a label like Noun or Verb for every word.

exercises.explain: POS Tagging looks at the context around a word to decide its part of speech. In 'cook the food', 'cook' is an action (Verb). In 'the cook is here', 'cook' is a person (Noun)!
